difficulty_evidence.multi_file: The fix changes four source files: Bash, Fish, and Zsh shell alias implementations plus CorrectedCommand logging in the shared types module.
difficulty_evidence.cross_function: The defect requires consistent environment-variable placement across Bash.app_alias, Fish.app_alias, and Zsh.app_alias; the target behavior is distributed across multiple shell-specific functions rather than one local statement.

difficulty_evidence.multi_file: The production fix changes both thefuck/shells/bash.py and thefuck/shells/zsh.py, while its regression test changes tests/shells/test_bash.py.
difficulty_evidence.cross_function: The fix coordinates Bash.app_alias, Bash.get_aliases, and Zsh.get_aliases: the generated alias exports TF_SHELL_ALIASES and both shell readers must consume the environment value without invoking an interactive Bash process.

The defect crosses _get_operations and _parse_operations in thefuck/rules/dnf_no_such_command.py: subprocess stdout arrives as bytes, while downstream matching must operate consistently on decoded text and a text regular expression.

difficulty_evidence.root_error_separated: The redirect assertion fails in HTTP client behavior while the causal body/header state is written in HTTP1Connection.
difficulty_evidence.dataflow: Method and body state propagate through redirect handling into connection output.

rejection_evidence.summary: This historical Tornado line is retained as a rejected case because the repository-wide suite is not a clean oracle on the benchmark-declared Python 3.7 runtime; the same project line produced broad runtime-compatibility failures during independent reproduction.

difficulty_evidence.dataflow: Parsed range bounds flow through normalization and response-status selection.
difficulty_evidence.high_similarity_candidates: Several adjacent range-bound conditions can produce similar unsatisfiable-range symptoms.
